title: Use configuration value for ExitCommandDelay
body:
This PR resolves issue #13222. It is still blocked by [PR941](https://github.com/containers/common/pull/941), since it requires a vendor update. Still needs to be tested therefore.

Signed-off-by: Rover van der Noort <s.r.vandernoort@student.tudelft.nl>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: @rvandernoort thanks for the PR, but the tests are not terribly happy....
Yes I know since [941](https://github.com/containers/common/pull/941) is merged now I can fix this, if I'm correct!

Alright, so I vendored the common library as explained in the issue, but this leaves me with a few questions:
- Other library's also seem to have updated because of this action. I pushed everything that changed now, or should I only update the common package?
- I can't seem to find the correct `containers.conf` file that my local build uses, I tried the /usr/share/ one and the vendon/common one, but both don't result in a changed delay time. Am I in the correct place?
I'll fix the commits after I succesfully tested the feature

@rvandernoort: PR needs rebase.
